content.full_text:
PRIVATE INDUSTRY COUNCIL
2425 Bisso Lane, Suite 100
Concord, CA 94520
(415) 646-5239

EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE
TIMOTHY T. THOMASON
Chair
Alvarado Bank

STEVEN GIACOMI
Vice Chair
The Giacomi Group

BARBARA N. SHAW
Past Chair
Barbara Shaw Seminars

ROBERT MATHEWS
Member at Large
Dept. of Rehabilitation

WILLIAM MAZOTTI
Chair Oversight Committee
Pacific Gas & Electric Company

WILLIE L. WILLIAMS
Chair Proposal Committee
BABCO

THOMAS LaFLEUR
Chair Planning Committee
Pittsburg Econ. & Housing Dev. Corp.

UDA JOHNSON
Chair Marketing and Economic Development
Bank of America

DIRECTORS
John Allen
Anne Crisp
Julie Croce
Annel Grove
Ina Rae Lapum
Joe Partida
Michael Romeo
David Rossi
William R. Sharkey III
Ronald Stewart
Wanda Viviano
Paul R. Witkay

EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR
ARTHUR C. MINER

January 18, 1990

Mr. Jonathan Rothman, Attorney
Legal Division, Suite 800
428 "J" Street
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Rothman:

The purpose of this correspondence is to obtain legal clarification from the F.P.P.C. on Proposition Initiative #73 Mass Mailings, as it applies to generating summer jobs for youths.

For the past seven years, the "Summer Youth Jobs Program" sponsored by a private-sector donation, has helped over 18,576 young people (16-21 years old) find summer jobs.

The major thrust in making the SYJP Program a success for the youths, is to alert the business community via a Mass Mailing campaign spearheaded by the Board of Supervisors to City Mayors, Chamber of Commerce.

There is no political competition being displayed by our local elected officials; in fact, they are only doing what they were elected to do: 1) generate employment to the unemployed youths population 2) serve all citizens equally, etc.

Enclosed is a copy of the standard letter sent to all local employers on our mailing list. The body of the letter is copied onto respective City and/or Chamber letterhead and mailed to employers, who in turn submit their work orders to any one of nine job placement offices located throughout the County.

These job placement offices are staffed with youths hired and compensated from private-sector funding. The youths are supervised by Employment Development Department staff who match-up work orders with youths seeking summer jobs.

Please provide me with your interpretation and legal written ruling on this matter, as preparation of launching the Summer Youth Jobs Program depends on it. Should you need further information, please contact me at (415) 646-5244.

Sincerely,

Pete R. Zaragoza
Summer Youth Jobs Coordinator
Contra Costa County Private Industry Council

Enclosure

cc: Arthur C. Miner, Executive Director
     Fred Canale, Marketing Director

PZ:kw

SAMPLE
City Letter

April 1989

Dear Employer:

Are you confronted with the task of hiring help to fill in for vacationing employees or need to hire temporary employees during the busy summer season? If so, we in the (name of city) have the answer to your need for summer help.

HIRE-A-YOUTH through the SUMMER YOUTH JOBS PROGRAM. It can save you time, money and the frustrations of hiring temporary or part-time help. The summer job placement center listed below has the names of eager young people ready to roll up their sleeves and go to work. They need the job you can provide. Given the chance, you will find them to be enthusiastic, productive workers.

Since 1983, the summer jobs program has helped 15,729 young people in the county to find summer jobs. With your job pledge, we can make this another great year for one of our greatest resource, youths.

If you know now that you can hire a young person for the summer, call the (Name of the youth job center) at (phone number) for referral of a qualified youth. If you have not made a decision, please look around to see if you have a job that needs to be done, and call.

Please join us in this worthwhile effort to help young people. Remember, any job you register with the program will help youths. "REACH OUT AND PUT A TEEN ON YOUR TEAM".

______________________________           ________________________________
Name, Mayor                                 Name, Supervisor District
City of (Name)                             County Board of Supervisors

______________________________           ________________________________
Name, President                           Name, Chair
(Name) Chamber of Commerce                 Private Industry Council
